name: Blackened (Cajun) Arctic Char
recipeInstructions:
1. Fillet the Arctic char (fresh or thawed) to a thickness of about 25 mm (1-1/2").
2. Feel free to substitute trout, perch, shrimp, or any firm-fleshed seafood or fish for the Arctic char.
3. Melt butter in a cast-iron or heavy-bottomed frying pan over medium-high heat.
4. Add Cajun spice and stir until heated thoroughly.
5. Place fillets in the pan and squeeze juice of one lemon into the pan.
6. Cook the fish for about 5 minutes on each side.
7. Serve with remaining lemon wedges and pan drippings.
Note: This recipe was originally published in the Calgary Herald (89.05.03) by Terry Bullick, who worked as an assistant cook at the Arctic Char Lodge, a fishing resort on Great Bear Lake, just 18 km south of the Arctic circle.
